howItWorks.intro: This rv solar panel calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.
rules: Daily Wh = Daily Ah × System Voltage
Solar Watts = Daily Wh ÷ Peak Sun Hours ÷ 0.85 (efficiency)
Battery Bank = Daily Ah × 2 (for 50% DoD and 1 day reserve)
howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates based on standard formulas. Verify with current local data for your specific situation.

faq.question: How many solar panels does an RV need?
faq.answer: Most RVers need 200-600W of solar. Light users (LED lights, phone charging): 200W. Moderate (TV, fridge): 400W. Heavy (AC, microwave): 800W+. Start by calculating your daily amp-hour usage.

faq.question: What are peak sun hours?
faq.answer: Peak sun hours represent the equivalent hours of full-intensity sunlight per day. In the US Southwest: 6-7 hours. Pacific Northwest: 3-4 hours. National average: 4-5 hours.

faq.question: Can I run AC on RV solar?
faq.answer: A small AC unit draws 1,200-1,500W. To run AC, you'd need at least 1,500W of solar with a large lithium battery bank (400+ Ah). It's possible but expensive. Most RVers use solar for everything except AC.

faq.question: Is the RV Solar Panel Calculator free to use?
faq.answer: Yes, completely free with no signup required. Use it as many times as you need — there are no limits or hidden fees.

faq.question: How accurate is this calculator?
faq.answer: This calculator uses standard practical calculation formulas trusted by users. Results are reliable estimates for planning purposes. For critical decisions, we recommend consulting a qualified professional to verify.

sections.title: What Is the RV Solar Panel Calculator?
sections.body: The RV Solar Panel Calculator is a free online tool designed for users who need quick, accurate calculations in the practical calculation space. By entering your daily power use, peak sun hours, battery voltage, you get instant results including solar watts needed, 100w panels needed, battery bank. No formulas to memorize, no spreadsheets to build — just enter your numbers and get the answer in seconds. Whether you're a beginner or experienced professional, this calculator saves you time and eliminates guesswork.

sections.title: Why This Calculation Matters
sections.body: Getting solar watts needed right can make the difference between success and costly mistakes. In practical calculation, small errors compound quickly. Manual calculations are error-prone and time-consuming, especially under pressure. This calculator applies proven formulas used by users worldwide, giving you confidence that your numbers are correct. Use it to get accurate results with precision and avoid common pitfalls that trip up beginners.

sections.title: When Should You Use This Calculator?
sections.body: This tool is most useful when you know your daily power use and need to find the right solar watts needed. It's also great for quick estimates before committing to a decision, and to double-check manual calculations or professional quotes, and when comparing different scenarios side by side. Bookmark this page and come back whenever you need a fast, reliable answer — the calculator is always free and requires no signup.

tips: Double-check your daily power use before calculating — even small input errors can significantly change your results.
Run the calculator with different values to compare scenarios and find the optimal approach for your situation.
Pay attention to both solar watts needed and 100w panels needed — they work together to give you the full picture.

If you're unsure about your battery voltage, start with a conservative estimate and adjust from there.
